title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis
text: can install development version afpca GitHub :

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis
text: examples running adaptive FPCA. details use package can found XYZ. code uses function afpca::simulate_adaptive_functional_data() simulate 40 curves Yi(t),  = 1, …, 20 observed 200 time points common grid domain (0,1) gaussian noise. functions generated mean function two functional principal components varying temporal smoothness defined : μ(t) = t−3/2 sin (π×t1/4)(t>1/2) ϕk(t) = t−3/2 sin (4π×k×t1/4)(t>1/2), k = 1, 2 plot show simulated data looks like:  software performs adaptively-smoothed functional principal component analysis. main function package afpca:fpca.adapt. code illustrates : plots show estimated mean function functional principal components  plots shows two examples observed functions respective reconstructions.
code: library(afpca)  simulated_data <- simulate_adaptive_functional_data(N.subj = 20) afpca.output <- fpca.adapt(data = simulated_data)

what: afpca: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis — afpca-package
title: afpca: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis — afpca-package
text: implementation adaptively smootheded functional principal component analysis.

title: afpca: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis — afpca-package
text: Maintainer: Angel Garcia de la Garza ag3914@columbia.edu (ORCID) Authors: Jeff Goldsmith jeff.goldsmith@columbia.edu Britton Sauerbrei bxs561@case.edu

what: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis — fpca.adapt
title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis — fpca.adapt
text: Decomposes functional observations using adaptive functional principal component analysis. method incorporates adaptive smoothness estimated decomposition using penalized likelihood framework

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis — fpca.adapt
code: fpca.adapt(   data,   basis = "trunc.poly",   poly.degree = 2,   knots = NA,   nbs = 35,   n.comp = 18,   pve = 0.99,   normalize.scores = TRUE,   orthogonalize_scores = TRUE,   orthogonalize_fpcs = TRUE,   ntimes = 100 )

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis — fpca.adapt
text: data data frame, matrix, tidyfun "tfd.reg" object functional observations. basis Type spline basis. "os" B-spline basis "trunc.poly" truncated polynomials (default) poly.degree degree spline basis knots Number knots used generate spline basis estimate mean function functional principal components nbs Number spline basis generate mean function functional principal components n.comp prespecified number principal components pve proportion variance explained; used choose number functional principal components normalize.scores normalize scores iteration (default true, recommended) orthogonalize_scores orthogonalize scores iteration (default true, recommended) orthogonalize_fpcs orthogonalize FPCs iteration (default true, recommended) ntimes Maximum number iterations

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis — fpca.adapt
text: function returns object class "afpca" contains following: Y observed data. Y_hat reconstructed fitted values mean vector estimated mean function fpcs tp (number timepoints) npc (number fpcs) matrix estimated eigenfunctions scores n (number subjects) npc (number fpcs) matrix estimated scores eigenvalues estimated eigenvalues .e. variances fpc scores Basis list containing penalized (Theta_beta) penalized (Theta_b) spline basis Theta concatenation (Theta_beta) penalized (Theta_b) spline basis coef estimated coefficients, coef %*% theta = fpcs convergence logical, algorithm converged .iter number iterations needed convergence

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis — fpca.adapt
text: Paper

title: Adaptive Functional Principal Component Analysis — fpca.adapt
code: sim_data <- simulate_adaptive_functional_data(n.tp = 100) afpca_output <- fpca.adapt(data = sim_data, nbs = 10)

text: data input data basis Type spline basis. "os" B-spline basis "trunc.poly" truncated polynomials (default) poly.degree Degree spline basis knots passed AdaptFitOS nbs number spline basis

title: Simulate Adaptive Functional Data — simulate_adaptive_functional_data
text: Simulated functional data includes sharp changes domain function (.e. smoothness rapidly changes domain). data simulates data using sinusoidal functions varying period amplitude.

title: Simulate Adaptive Functional Data — simulate_adaptive_functional_data
code: simulate_adaptive_functional_data(   n.tp = 200,   N.subj = 20,   var.scores = c(4, 1),   noise.var = 0.05,   seed.num = 1 )

title: Simulate Adaptive Functional Data — simulate_adaptive_functional_data
text: n.tp number timepoints N.subj number subjects var.scores variance scores (value eigenvalues) noise.var residual noise variance seed.num seed number

title: Simulate Adaptive Functional Data — simulate_adaptive_functional_data
text: object class "fpca_sim_data" contains following: data simulated dataset data_true simulated dataset without noise Mu_true true data-generating mean-function Phi_true true data-generating functional principal components Scores_true true scores

title: Simulate Adaptive Functional Data — simulate_adaptive_functional_data
